evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Establish data governance, testing, and quality frameworks
evidence.domain_risk: Build and maintain scalable dbt models on Snowflake for cross-entity analytics
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and maintain scalable dbt models on Snowflake for cross-entity analytics
evidence.jd_authorship: Build and maintain scalable dbt models on Snowflake for cross-entity analytics
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Enable Data Analysts to deliver insights through reliable, documented dbt models
evidence.autonomy_level: Establish data governance, testing, and quality frameworks
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and maintain scalable dbt models; Establish data governance, testing, and quality frameworks
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Building scalable dbt models is greenfield-leaning work, while establishing governance and testing frameworks alongside maintenance responsibilities supports the mixed classification.
evidence.work_arrangement: Berlin or Copenhagen (hybrid: 2 remote days/week possible)
evidence.ai_role: Drive AI-assisted workflows, including GitHub Copilot integration
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to drive adoption of AI coding tools like GitHub Copilot to accelerate their own analytics engineering work, which is the ai_user pattern rather than AI-infrastructure enablement.
evidence.testing_framing: Establish data governance, testing, and quality frameworks
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The verb 'Establish' assigns the candidate direct ownership of building testing and quality frameworks rather than merely listing a testing tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Enable Data Analysts to deliver insights through reliable, documented dbt models
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The concern centers on pipeline reliability and unblocking downstream analysts rather than compliance or regulatory trust, supporting a moderate operational-reliability reading.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple rigour signals (governance, testing, quality, documentation, peer review) with no velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Data errors affect business analytics and decisions but no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language present.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Snowflake) in responsibilities with existing Data Engineering and Analytics teams indicate established function beyond greenfield.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Technically specific responsibilities naming tools with application context suggest hiring manager input, but generic boilerplate language elsewhere ('collaborate', 'establish') indicates recruiter involvement.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role primarily serves the analytics function through infrastructure work, not commercial, product, or finance teams.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role genuinely combines strategic domain ownership (governance, standards) with execution (building models, supporting analysts).
